title: Cross-fitted hierarchical target encoding for strategy identities
summary: Your strongest runs still use category codes/frequencies for `Driver`, `Race`, and `Compound`.
rationale: Your strongest runs still use category codes/frequencies for `Driver`, `Race`, and `Compound`. With 800+ drivers and race-specific behavior, leakage-safe target encodings can capture stable pit priors that ordinal codes miss. scikit-learn explicitly warns that target encoding without cross-fitting overfits high-cardinality features.
implementation_hint: Build OOF encodings under the same race-grouped folds for `Driver`, `Race`, `Compound`, plus interactions like `DriverĂ—Compound`, `RaceĂ—Compound`, `YearĂ—Race`, `DriverĂ—Race`. Use shrinkage toward global mean and optionally toward parent means (for example `DriverĂ—Compound` -> `Driver` -> global). Apply learned mappings to validation/test only from training folds.
expected_effect: Better use of high-cardinality entities and compound/race interactions with low implementation cost; likely small but real AUC lift if current codes are underfitting.
risk: If any encoding is fit on full data or ignores race grouping, CV will inflate badly. Gains may vanish if CatBoost already captures most categorical signal.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
